prompt:
A generic medical-product instruction illustration in simple black line-art on a pure white background, in the universal patient-information-leaflet style. Subject: a hand actively PEELING the paper-foil seal off a small sealed medical pouch — captured MID-PEEL.

SCALE: the pouch is SMALL relative to the hand — the hand is the larger element in the frame, the pouch fits comfortably in the palm-area. The hand should clearly read as bigger than the pouch.

POUCH SHAPE: the pouch hugs the circular white device inside tightly — a thin (~3-5mm) foil flange traces the device's circular silhouette. Small lot mark on the top edge of the flange.

FOIL OPACITY: where the foil is still attached and flat, it's OPAQUE — you do NOT see the device underneath. Only the peeled-back area exposes the device.

DEVICE INSIDE: a plain round white plastic disc — smooth, featureless on its main face. A small spout / connector protrudes from the lower edge of the disc. DO NOT add any centered slot, slit, line, screw-head, button, or other circular detail in the middle of the disc — the disc face is plain.

ACTION: a single hand approaches from the upper-right; thumb and forefinger pinch the foil flap at the lower-front edge and pull it back / down in a soft curl, exposing roughly the lower half of the device underneath. The remaining top half of the foil stays flat and opaque, concealing the upper half of the device. Three-quarter angle.

Style: uniform medium-weight black ink lines on clean white, no color. Minimal short parallel hatching only along the curled-back foil's underside and the inner edge of the exposed device to suggest shadow. No frame, no border, no caption, no labels, no logos, no arrows, no decorative elements. Subject centered in a 1:1 square composition with generous white margins. Drawn-by-hand line work, generic patient information leaflet illustration style.
